critic_raw:
The data split uses stratified random sampling across 60 months of EHR data. For a 30-day readmission prediction task, this means patient encounters from later time periods can appear in the training set while encounters from earlier periods appear in the test set. Hospital discharge practices, coding standards, care protocols, and patient populations shift over 5 years. Training on 'future' encounters when predicting 'past' encounters is methodologically invalid for deployment simulation and produces optimistic performance estimates by allowing the model to learn from the statistical regime that includes the test period.

The multi-task model uses Clinical BERT embeddings (768-dim) concatenated with TF-IDF features from three note types plus a full clinical note text pipeline, while the baseline logistic regression is trained on structured features only—no clinical notes. The design describes this as 'baseline represents current standard practice,' but this means any performance advantage for the deep model could be entirely attributable to having access to unstructured text (a data modality advantage) rather than to the model architecture or multi-task learning. The hypothesis claims to test multi-task deep learning versus logistic regression; the actual test is text+structure+deep learning versus structure-only shallow learning.

The auxiliary task (LOS regression sharing the first two hidden layers) introduces a subtle regularization path: if length of stay is correlated with 30-day readmission (which it is clinically), then training the shared layers to predict LOS effectively allows the model to use LOS-predictive features for the readmission task through the shared representation. If LOS is also used as an explicit input feature (listed in structured features), this creates a double-dipping path where LOS information enters via both the input feature vector and the auxiliary training signal.

Model selection uses validation AUROC, while the stated primary metric for test evaluation is also AUROC — this is internally consistent. However, the operating threshold for sensitivity/specificity is 'selected on validation data to achieve ≥90% sensitivity' and reported on the test set. Selecting a threshold on validation data and applying it to test data is standard, but the threshold is highly sensitive to class prevalence and score distribution; if the validation set's score distribution differs from the test set's (possible with random splits of temporal data), the 90% sensitivity target may not be met on the test set.
all_issues_raised: Stratified random sampling across 60 months of EHR data allows future-era encounters to train on past-era predictions, invalidating the deployment simulation and inflating performance estimates.

The multi-task model receives Clinical BERT text embeddings plus full note text while the logistic regression baseline receives structured features only — the comparison confounds data modality access with model architecture, so any performance difference cannot be attributed to multi-task learning specifically.

Length of stay is both an explicit input feature and the target of the auxiliary regression task sharing the first two hidden layers, creating a double-dipping path where LOS signal enters the readmission prediction through both feature input and auxiliary training gradient.

The threshold for the 90% sensitivity operating point is selected on the validation set; with a random split of temporal data, the validation score distribution may not match the test set's distribution, and the threshold may not achieve the stated sensitivity target on test data.
all_issues_adjudicated: Stratified random sampling across 60 months of EHR data allows future-era encounters to train on past-era predictions, invalidating the deployment simulation and inflating performance estimates.

The multi-task model receives Clinical BERT text embeddings plus full note text while the logistic regression baseline receives structured features only — the comparison confounds data modality access with model architecture, so any performance difference cannot be attributed to multi-task learning specifically.

Length of stay is both an explicit input feature and the target of the auxiliary regression task sharing the first two hidden layers, creating a double-dipping path where LOS signal enters the readmission prediction through both feature input and auxiliary training gradient.

The threshold for the 90% sensitivity operating point is selected on the validation set; with a random split of temporal data, the validation score distribution may not match the test set's distribution, and the threshold may not achieve the stated sensitivity target on test data.
